summary:
The stylopharyngeus muscle is unique among pharyngeal muscles because it receives sole innervation from the glossopharyngeal nerve, making it completely independent of the cranial accessory nerve. As such, it remains fully functional after complete transection of the cranial accessory nerve, while all other pharyngeal muscles depend on vagal inputs affected indirectly by this structure.  

\boxed{C}

\boxed{C}. Answer choice: A
